domains.label: Brain
domains.definition: Features derived from brain imaging: structure (morphometry) and function (connectomics).

children.label: Morphometry
children.definition: Structural morphometry of the brain from T1-weighted MRI (FreeSurfer): regional volumes, cortical thickness and surface area.

children.label: Subcortical Volumes
children.definition: Volumes of key bilateral subcortical structures.

children.label: Cortical Thickness
children.definition: Mean cortical thickness of Desikan-Killiany regions.

children.label: Left banks of superior temporal sulcus thickness
Right banks of superior temporal sulcus thickness
Cortical Surface Area
children.definition: White-surface area of Desikan-Killiany cortical regions.

children.label: Left rostral middle frontal surface area
Left caudal middle frontal surface area
Right rostral middle frontal surface area
Right caudal middle frontal surface area
Left banks of superior temporal sulcus surface area
Right banks of superior temporal sulcus surface area
Left rostral anterior cingulate surface area
Left caudal anterior cingulate surface area
Right rostral anterior cingulate surface area
Right caudal anterior cingulate surface area
Cortical Gray-Matter Volume
children.definition: Gray-matter volume of Desikan-Killiany cortical regions.

children.label: Left rostral middle frontal gray-matter volume
Left caudal middle frontal gray-matter volume
Right rostral middle frontal gray-matter volume
Right caudal middle frontal gray-matter volume
Left banks of superior temporal sulcus gray-matter volume
Right banks of superior temporal sulcus gray-matter volume
Left rostral anterior cingulate gray-matter volume
Left caudal anterior cingulate gray-matter volume
Right rostral anterior cingulate gray-matter volume
Right caudal anterior cingulate gray-matter volume
Connectomics
children.definition: Functional connectivity of the brain from movie-watching fMRI (Yeo/Schaefer intrinsic networks).

children.label: Within-network FC
children.definition: Mean functional connectivity among parcels of the same intrinsic network.

children.label: Between-network FC
children.definition: Mean functional connectivity between pairs of intrinsic networks.

children.label: Dorsal attention - Salience / ventral attention FC
Salience / ventral attention - Frontoparietal control FC
Salience / ventral attention - Default mode FC

domains.label: Demographics and Anthropometrics
domains.definition: Basic biological and socio-economic characteristics of the participants, including age, sex, physical measurements, and educational background.

children.label: Biological Characteristics
children.definition: Fundamental physical and physiological attributes of the participant.

children.label: Body mass index
children.definition: Body mass index (kg/m^2). Zero values are data errors and treated as missing.

children.label: Socioeconomic Background
children.definition: Educational attainment and environmental socioeconomic status of the participant.

children.label: Education level
children.definition: Highest completed education level (CBS classification): low/medium/high.

children.label: Background socio-economic status
children.definition: Background SES from parental income and education (range 2-6).

domains.label: Psychological Constructs
domains.definition: Standardized psychometric measures assessing personality traits, reinforcement sensitivity, and trait anxiety.

children.label: NEO-FFI Big Five Personality
children.definition: Standardized psychometric measures assessing the five broad dimensions of personality.

children.label: Openness (NEO-FFI)
children.definition: NEO-FFI Openness to experience scale (sum of items).

children.label: BIS/BAS Reinforcement Sensitivity
children.definition: Psychometric scales measuring behavioral inhibition and activation systems related to reinforcement sensitivity.

children.label: BAS reward responsiveness
children.definition: Behavioural Activation System reward responsiveness subscale.

children.label: STAI Trait Anxiety
children.definition: Psychometric measure assessing stable individual differences in anxiety proneness.

domains.label: Identity and Belief Systems
domains.definition: Self-reported measures concerning sexual orientation, gender identity, and religious affiliation or importance.

children.label: Sexual and Gender Identity
children.definition: Self-reported measures assessing an individual's sexual attraction patterns and internal sense of gender identity.

children.label: Gender identity (male)
children.definition: Degree to which the subject felt male (1-7).

children.label: Gender identity (female)
children.definition: Degree to which the subject felt female (1-7).

children.label: Religiosity
children.definition: Self-reported measures concerning religious background, current religious status, and the perceived importance of religion in daily life.

children.label: Religious upbringing
children.definition: Whether the subject had a religious upbringing (yes/no).

children.label: Currently religious
children.definition: Whether the subject is religious now (yes/no).

children.label: Importance of religion
children.definition: How much religion plays a role in the subject's life (1-5).

context: AOMIC ID1000: 928 healthy Dutch young adults. The features grouped here are self-report / questionnaire measures: NEO-FFI Big Five personality, BIS/BAS reinforcement sensitivity, STAI trait anxiety, demographics, anthropometrics, socio-economic status, sexual/gender identity ratings and religiosity. Group measures from the same instrument or construct together. (Brain imaging features - FreeSurfer morphometry and the functional connectome - are placed deterministically under a single BRAIN domain via explicit paths and are not part of this semantic grouping step.)
user_guidance: Organise the self-report features into exactly three top-level domains: (1) 'Demographics and Anthropometrics' - age, sex, handedness, BMI, education and socio-economic background; (2) 'Psychological Constructs' - NEO-FFI Big Five personality, BIS/BAS reinforcement sensitivity, and STAI trait anxiety, each as its own subdomain; (3) 'Identity and Belief Systems' - sexual and gender identity ratings, and religiosity. Keep identity and belief separate from demographics.
